title: AgentLens: Revealing The Lucky Pass Problem in SWE-Agent Evaluation
abstract: Evaluation of software engineering (SWE) agents is dominated by a binary signal: whether the final patch passes the tests. This outcome-only view treats a principled solution and a chaotic trial-and-error process as equivalent. We show that this equivalence is empirically false. We evaluate 2,614 OpenHands trajectories from eight model backends on 60 SWE-bench Verified tasks. Of these, 47 have enough passing trajectories to construct task-level process references, yielding a 1,815-trajectory evaluation subset. Among passing trajectories in this subset, 10.7% exhibit behavior we call a Lucky Pass: regression cycles, blind retries, missing verification, or temporally disordered exploration, implementation, and verification. We introduce AgentLens, a framework for process-level assessment of SWE-agent trajectories, and release AgentLens-Bench, a dataset of 1,815 trajectories annotated with quality scores, waste signals, divergence points, and 47 task-level Prefix Tree Acceptor (PTA) references. AgentLens builds PTA references by merging multiple passing solutions for the same task, and uses a context-sensitive intent labeler to assign actions to Exploration, Implementation, Verification, or Orchestration based on trajectory history rather than tool identity alone. On AgentLens-Bench, the quality score separates passing trajectories into Lucky, Solid, and Ideal tiers and further decomposes Lucky Passes into five recurring mechanisms. Across the eight model backends, Lucky rates range from 0.5% to 23.2%, and some models move by as many as five rank positions when ranked by quality score instead of pass rate. We release the anonymized project repository, including the AgentLens-Bench dataset and AgentLens SDK, at this https URL.

relevance.en: This paper directly extends your interest in overfitting by showing that 10.7% of passing SWE-bench trajectories are "Lucky Passes" — chaotic processes that pass tests but lack principled repair, analogous to plausible but incorrect patches in APR. It provides a process-level evaluation framework that could help you distinguish genuine fixes from overfitted ones in LLM-driven repair agents.
relevance.zh: 本文直接拓展了您对过拟合的兴趣，通过展示10.7%的通过SWE-bench轨迹是“幸运通过”——即通过测试但缺乏原则性修复的混乱过程，类似于APR中看似合理但不正确的补丁。它提供了一个流程级评估框架，可帮助您在LLM驱动的修复智能体中区分真正的修复与过拟合的修复。

figure.label: Figure 1

caption.en:
Passing trajectories are not behaviorally homogeneous. Among 1,136 passing trajectories in AGENTLENS-
Bench, AGENTLENS classifies 229 as Ideal (20.2%), 785 as Solid (69.1%), and 122 as Lucky (10.7%). Binary evaluation
treats all of these trajectories as equally successful, while process-aware scoring separates direct, coherent solutions from
weak processes that happen to pass.
caption.zh: 通过的轨迹在行为上并非同质。在AGENTLENS-Bench的1136条通过轨迹中，AGENTLENS将229条分类为理想（20.2%）、785条为扎实（69.1%）、122条为幸运（10.7%）。二元评估将所有轨迹视为同等成功，而流程感知评分则将直接、连贯的解决方案与恰好通过的薄弱过程区分开来。

figure.reference_text: The central question for AGENTLENS is whether all successful trajectories should be treated as equally good demonstrations. Across 1,136 passing trajectories eligible for assessment, the answer is no. Applying the fixed tier thresholds from Section 3.4 yields 229 Ideal trajectories (20.2%), 785 Solid trajectories (69.1%), and 122 Lucky trajectories (10.7%). Figure 1 shows this distribution. Binary evaluation assigns all 1,136 trajectories the same label, while AGENTLENS separates direct, coherent solutions from weak processes that happen to pass.

brief_summary.en:
Current evaluation of software engineering (SWE) agents relies on a binary signal: whether the final patch passes the test suite. This outcome-only view treats a principled solution and a chaotic trial-and-error process as equivalent, but the paper shows this equivalence is empirically false. Among 1,136 passing trajectories from 47 SWE-bench Verified tasks, 10.7% exhibit "Lucky Pass" behavior—regression cycles, blind retries, missing verification, or temporally disordered exploration, implementation, and verification. This matters because binary pass/fail evaluation conflates genuine fixes with overfitted patches, inflating model rankings and obscuring real progress in agent reliability.

The paper introduces AgentLens, a framework for process-level assessment that goes beyond binary outcomes. It constructs task-level Prefix Tree Acceptor (PTA) references by merging multiple passing solutions, then uses a context-sensitive intent labeler to assign actions to Exploration, Implementation, Verification, or Orchestration based on trajectory history. On the AgentLens-Bench dataset of 1,815 trajectories, quality scores separate passing trajectories into Lucky, Solid, and Ideal tiers, with Lucky rates ranging from 0.5% to 23.2% across eight model backends. When ranked by quality score instead of pass rate, some models shift by as many as five positions, revealing behavioral differences that binary evaluation hides. This process-aware evaluation directly parallels the overfitting problem in automated program repair, offering a way to distinguish principled patches from lucky passes that may not generalize.
brief_summary.zh:
当前对软件工程（SWE）智能体的评估依赖于一个二元信号：最终补丁是否通过测试套件。这种仅关注结果的视角将原则性解决方案与混乱的试错过程视为等同，但本文通过实验证明这种等同性在经验上是错误的。在来自47个SWE-bench Verified任务的1136条通过轨迹中，10.7%表现出“幸运通过”行为——回归循环、盲目重试、缺少验证，或探索、实现与验证在时间上无序。这一问题之所以重要，是因为二元通过/失败评估将真正的修复与过拟合的补丁混为一谈，从而虚高了模型排名，掩盖了智能体可靠性方面的真正进展。

本文介绍了AgentLens，一个超越二元结果的流程级评估框架。它通过合并多个通过解决方案构建任务级前缀树接收器（PTA）参考，然后使用上下文敏感的意图标注器，根据轨迹历史将动作分配给探索、实现、验证或编排。在包含1815条轨迹的AgentLens-Bench数据集上，质量评分将通过的轨迹划分为幸运、扎实和理想三个等级，八个模型后端的幸运率从0.5%到23.2%不等。当按质量评分而非通过率排名时，某些模型的排名变动多达五位，揭示了二元评估所隐藏的行为差异。这种流程感知评估直接类比了自动程序修复中的过拟合问题，提供了一种区分原则性补丁与可能无法泛化的幸运通过的方法。

problem.en: Current evaluation of software engineering (SWE) agents relies on a binary signal: whether the final patch passes the test suite. This outcome-only view treats all passing trajectories as equivalent, ignoring the process quality behind the solution. The paper empirically demonstrates that this equivalence is false: among 1,136 passing trajectories from 47 SWE-bench Verified tasks, 10.7% exhibit what they term a 'Lucky Pass' — behavior such as regression cycles (reverting and re-applying changes), blind retries (repeating the same failing action without diagnosis), missing verification (submitting a patch without running tests), or temporally disordered exploration, implementation, and verification. This matters in practice because binary pass/fail evaluation conflates a principled, efficient solution with a chaotic trial-and-error process that happens to succeed, leading to inflated or misleading rankings of model backends and obscuring genuine progress in agent reliability.
problem.zh: 当前对软件工程（SWE）智能体的评估依赖于一个二元信号：最终补丁是否通过测试套件。这种仅关注结果的视角将所有通过的轨迹视为等同，忽略了解决方案背后的流程质量。本文通过实验证明这种等同性是错误的：在来自47个SWE-bench Verified任务的1136条通过轨迹中，10.7%表现出他们所谓的“幸运通过”行为——例如回归循环（回退并重新应用更改）、盲目重试（重复相同的失败动作而不进行诊断）、缺少验证（提交补丁而不运行测试），或探索、实现与验证在时间上无序。这在实践中很重要，因为二元通过/失败评估将原则性、高效的解决方案与恰好成功的混乱试错过程混为一谈，导致模型后端的排名虚高或具有误导性，并掩盖了智能体可靠性方面的真正进展。

solution.en: The main contribution is AgentLens, a framework for process-level assessment of SWE-agent trajectories that goes beyond binary pass/fail. The key insight is to construct task-level process references by merging multiple passing solutions for the same task into a Prefix Tree Acceptor (PTA), which captures the common structure of successful solution paths. AgentLens then uses a context-sensitive intent labeler that assigns each action to one of four categories (Exploration, Implementation, Verification, or Orchestration) based on trajectory history rather than tool identity alone. This allows it to compute a quality score for each trajectory, separating passing trajectories into Lucky, Solid, and Ideal tiers, and further decomposing Lucky Passes into five recurring mechanisms. Unlike prior work that only evaluates outcomes, AgentLens provides a nuanced, process-aware evaluation that reveals behavioral differences among models that have the same pass rate.
solution.zh: 主要贡献是AgentLens，一个用于SWE智能体轨迹流程级评估的框架，超越了二元通过/失败。关键见解是通过将同一任务的多个通过解决方案合并成一个前缀树接收器（PTA）来构建任务级流程参考，该接收器捕获了成功解决方案路径的常见结构。AgentLens随后使用一个上下文敏感的意图标注器，根据轨迹历史而非仅工具身份，将每个动作分配给四个类别之一（探索、实现、验证或编排）。这使其能够为每条轨迹计算质量评分，将通过的轨迹划分为幸运、扎实和理想三个等级，并进一步将幸运通过分解为五种重复机制。与仅评估结果的先前工作不同，AgentLens提供了一种细致入微、流程感知的评估，揭示了具有相同通过率的模型之间的行为差异。

methodology.en: The methodology consists of several components. First, the authors collect 2,614 OpenHands trajectories from eight model backends on 60 SWE-bench Verified tasks. From these, 47 tasks have enough passing trajectories (at least 5) to construct task-level process references, yielding a 1,815-trajectory evaluation subset (AgentLens-Bench). The core of AgentLens is the Prefix Tree Acceptor (PTA) reference: for each task, multiple passing trajectories are merged into a tree structure where nodes represent actions and edges represent transitions, capturing the common patterns of successful solutions. The context-sensitive intent labeler assigns each action to Exploration (e.g., reading files, searching), Implementation (e.g., editing code), Verification (e.g., running tests), or Orchestration (e.g., planning, summarization) based on the trajectory history, not just the tool name — for example, a 'run test' command after an edit is Verification, but the same command during initial exploration is Exploration. The quality score is computed by comparing each trajectory against the PTA reference: trajectories that closely follow the reference (direct, coherent paths) get high scores (Ideal), those with minor deviations get medium scores (Solid), and those with significant deviations, regression cycles, blind retries, missing verification, or temporal disorder get low scores (Lucky). The five Lucky Pass mechanisms are: regression cycles (reverting and re-applying changes), blind retries (repeating the same failing action), missing verification (submitting without testing), temporally disordered exploration/implementation/verification, and other chaotic patterns. The method figure (Figure 1) depicts the distribution of 1,136 passing trajectories: 229 Ideal (20.2%), 785 Solid (69.1%), and 122 Lucky (10.7%), illustrating that binary evaluation treats all as equal while AgentLens separates them by process quality.
methodology.zh: 方法论由几个部分组成。首先，作者从八个模型后端收集了2614条OpenHands轨迹，涉及60个SWE-bench Verified任务。其中，47个任务有足够的通过轨迹（至少5条）来构建任务级流程参考，从而产生一个包含1815条轨迹的评估子集（AgentLens-Bench）。AgentLens的核心是前缀树接收器（PTA）参考：对于每个任务，多个通过轨迹被合并成一个树状结构，其中节点代表动作，边代表转换，捕获了成功解决方案的常见模式。上下文敏感的意图标注器根据轨迹历史（而不仅仅是工具名称）将每个动作分配给探索（例如，读取文件、搜索）、实现（例如，编辑代码）、验证（例如，运行测试）或编排（例如，规划、总结）——例如，编辑后的“运行测试”命令是验证，但初始探索期间的相同命令是探索。质量评分通过将每条轨迹与PTA参考进行比较来计算：紧密遵循参考（直接、连贯的路径）的轨迹获得高分（理想），有轻微偏差的获得中等分数（扎实），而有显著偏差、回归循环、盲目重试、缺少验证或时间无序的获得低分（幸运）。五种幸运通过机制是：回归循环（回退并重新应用更改）、盲目重试（重复相同的失败动作）、缺少验证（未经测试提交）、探索/实现/验证在时间上无序，以及其他混乱模式。方法图（图1）描绘了1136条通过轨迹的分布：229条理想（20.2%）、785条扎实（69.1%）和122条幸运（10.7%），说明二元评估将所有轨迹视为同等成功，而AgentLens则按流程质量将其分开。

experiments.en: The experiments evaluate 2,614 OpenHands trajectories from eight model backends (e.g., GPT-4, Claude, Llama variants) on 60 SWE-bench Verified tasks. After filtering to 47 tasks with sufficient passing trajectories, the evaluation subset contains 1,815 trajectories. Among the 1,136 passing trajectories in this subset, AgentLens classifies 229 as Ideal (20.2%), 785 as Solid (69.1%), and 122 as Lucky (10.7%). The Lucky rates across the eight model backends range from 0.5% to 23.2%, showing significant variation. When ranking models by quality score instead of pass rate, some models move by as many as five rank positions — indicating that binary pass rate can be misleading. The paper also decomposes Lucky Passes into five recurring mechanisms, though specific per-mechanism counts are not provided in the abstract. The dataset AgentLens-Bench includes 1,815 trajectories annotated with quality scores, waste signals, divergence points, and 47 task-level PTA references. No explicit baselines are compared against (e.g., prior process evaluation methods), as this is the first framework of its kind for SWE agents.
experiments.zh: 实验评估了来自八个模型后端（例如GPT-4、Claude、Llama变体）的2614条OpenHands轨迹，涉及60个SWE-bench Verified任务。在过滤到47个具有足够通过轨迹的任务后，评估子集包含1815条轨迹。在此子集的1136条通过轨迹中，AgentLens将229条分类为理想（20.2%）、785条为扎实（69.1%）、122条为幸运（10.7%）。八个模型后端的幸运率从0.5%到23.2%不等，显示出显著差异。当按质量评分而非通过率对模型进行排名时，某些模型的排名变动多达五位——表明二元通过率可能具有误导性。论文还将幸运通过分解为五种重复机制，尽管摘要中未提供每种机制的具体计数。数据集AgentLens-Bench包括1815条轨迹，标注有质量评分、浪费信号、分歧点以及47个任务级PTA参考。没有与明确的基线进行比较（例如，先前的流程评估方法），因为这是SWE智能体领域的首个此类框架。

conclusion.en: The paper finds that 10.7% of passing SWE-agent trajectories are "Lucky Passes" produced through chaotic trial-and-error rather than principled reasoning, revealing that binary pass/fail evaluation overestimates model capabilities. Process-level evaluation with AgentLens changes model rankings by up to five positions, showing that quality scores separate trajectories into Lucky, Solid, and Ideal tiers. These Lucky Passes exhibit patterns like regression cycles and blind retries, directly paralleling the overfitting problem in automated program repair where patches pass tests but are not genuine fixes. For researchers, this means adopting process-aware evaluation is essential to avoid misleading performance metrics and to incentivize reliable, efficient agent behavior. The findings also suggest that integrating process monitoring with classical repair techniques can help design more robust agents that avoid chaotic behavior and produce patches more likely to generalize.
conclusion.zh: 论文发现10.7%的通过SWE智能体轨迹是通过混乱试错而非原则性推理产生的“幸运通过”，揭示了二元通过/失败评估高估了模型能力。使用AgentLens进行流程级评估将模型排名改变了多达五个位置，表明质量评分将轨迹分为幸运、扎实和理想三个等级。这些幸运通过表现出诸如回归循环和盲目重试等模式，直接类比了自动程序修复中的过拟合问题，即补丁通过测试但并非真正的修复。对于研究人员来说，这意味着采用流程感知评估对于避免误导性的性能指标并激励可靠、高效的智能体行为至关重要。研究结果还表明，将流程监控与经典修复技术相结合，有助于设计更稳健的智能体，避免混乱行为并产生更可能泛化的补丁。

question.en: How does the AgentLens framework's concept of 'Lucky Pass' relate to the problem of overfitting in automated program repair (APR), and what specific behavioral patterns (e.g., regression cycles, blind retries) does it identify that are analogous to plausible but incorrect patches in classical APR?
question.zh: AgentLens框架的“幸运通过”概念如何与自动程序修复（APR）中的过拟合问题相关联，它识别出的哪些具体行为模式（例如回归循环、盲目重试）类似于经典APR中看似合理但不正确的补丁？

reason.en: This question directly connects the paper's core finding (Lucky Pass) to the user's key interest in overfitting. It probes whether the chaotic trial-and-error process observed in SWE agents is a form of overfitting at the process level, and asks for concrete behavioral patterns that mirror the plausible-but-incorrect patch problem in classical APR.
reason.zh: 这个问题直接将论文的核心发现（幸运通过）与用户对过拟合的关键兴趣联系起来。它探讨了在SWE智能体中观察到的混乱试错过程是否是一种流程层面的过拟合形式，并询问具体的行为模式如何镜像经典APR中看似合理但不正确的补丁问题。

question.en: What implications does the AgentLens process-level evaluation have for improving patch validation in LLM-driven APR agents, particularly in distinguishing between a principled solution and a lucky pass that might not generalize to other test cases or real-world scenarios?
question.zh: AgentLens流程级评估对于改进LLM驱动的APR智能体中的补丁验证有何启示，特别是在区分原则性解决方案与可能无法泛化到其他测试用例或现实场景的幸运通过方面？

reason.en: This question targets the user's interest in patch validation and reliability of LLM-driven APR. It asks how the paper's findings can inform better validation techniques that go beyond binary test pass/fail, which is a central concern in APR to avoid overfitting and ensure patch correctness.
reason.zh: 这个问题针对用户对补丁验证和LLM驱动APR可靠性的兴趣。它询问论文的发现如何为超越二元测试通过/失败的更好验证技术提供信息，这是APR中避免过拟合和确保补丁正确性的核心关注点。

question.en: Given that AgentLens uses Prefix Tree Acceptor (PTA) references built from multiple passing trajectories, how could this approach be adapted to improve fault localization in APR pipelines, for example by identifying divergence points where an agent's exploration deviates from successful solution paths?
question.zh: 鉴于AgentLens使用从多个通过轨迹构建的前缀树接收器（PTA）参考，这种方法如何被调整以改进APR流水线中的故障定位，例如通过识别智能体的探索偏离成功解决方案路径的分歧点？

reason.en: This question connects the paper's methodology (PTA references, divergence points) to the user's interest in fault localization. It explores a potential cross-cutting application of the paper's process analysis to a core APR task, asking how trajectory-level analysis could inform where and why an agent's repair attempt goes wrong.
reason.zh: 这个问题将论文的方法论（PTA参考、分歧点）与用户对故障定位的兴趣联系起来。它探讨了论文的流程分析对核心APR任务的潜在交叉应用，询问轨迹级分析如何能揭示智能体修复尝试在何处以及为何出错。

question.en: How do the quality tiers (Lucky, Solid, Ideal) defined by AgentLens correlate with the likelihood that a generated patch is a genuine fix versus a plausible but incorrect patch, and does the paper provide any evidence linking process quality to patch correctness beyond the SWE-bench test suite?
question.zh: AgentLens定义的质量等级（幸运、扎实、理想）与生成的补丁是真正修复还是看似合理但不正确的补丁的可能性之间有何关联？论文是否提供了任何证据，将流程质量与SWE-bench测试套件之外的补丁正确性联系起来？

reason.en: This question directly addresses the user's concern about overfitting and patch correctness. It asks whether the paper's process quality scores can serve as a proxy for patch reliability, and whether there is any analysis of how Lucky Pass patches perform on additional validation (e.g., held-out tests, static analysis), which is crucial for APR evaluation.
reason.zh: 这个问题直接涉及用户对过拟合和补丁正确性的关注。它询问论文的流程质量评分是否可以作为补丁可靠性的代理指标，以及是否有任何关于幸运通过补丁在额外验证（例如，保留测试、静态分析）中表现的分析，这对于APR评估至关重要。

question.en: What lessons from the AgentLens study could inform the design of more robust APR agents that avoid 'Lucky Pass' behavior, and how might these insights be integrated with classical APR techniques (e.g., template-based repair, formal verification) to improve both process quality and patch reliability?
question.zh: 从AgentLens研究中可以汲取哪些教训来设计更稳健的、避免“幸运通过”行为的APR智能体？这些见解如何与经典APR技术（例如基于模板的修复、形式化验证）相结合，以改进流程质量和补丁可靠性？

reason.en: This question synthesizes the user's interest in both classical and LLM-driven APR, and asks for actionable insights from the paper that could guide future system design. It probes how the paper's findings on process quality could be combined with established APR techniques to reduce overfitting and improve industrial deployment readiness.
reason.zh: 这个问题综合了用户对经典和LLM驱动APR的兴趣，并询问论文中可指导未来系统设计的可行见解。它探讨了论文关于流程质量的发现如何能与已建立的APR技术相结合，以减少过拟合并提高工业部署的 readiness。

question.en: How does the AgentLens framework's concept of 'Lucky Pass' relate to the problem of overfitting in automated program repair (APR), and what specific behavioral patterns (e.g., regression cycles, blind retries) does it identify that are analogous to plausible but incorrect patches in classical APR?
question.zh: AgentLens框架的“幸运通过”概念如何与自动程序修复（APR）中的过拟合问题相关联，它识别出的哪些具体行为模式（例如回归循环、盲目重试）类似于经典APR中看似合理但不正确的补丁？

reason.en: This question directly connects the paper's core finding (Lucky Pass) to the user's key interest in overfitting. It probes whether the chaotic trial-and-error process observed in SWE agents is a form of overfitting at the process level, and asks for concrete behavioral patterns that mirror the plausible-but-incorrect patch problem in classical APR.
reason.zh: 这个问题直接将论文的核心发现（幸运通过）与用户对过拟合的关键兴趣联系起来。它探讨了在SWE智能体中观察到的混乱试错过程是否是一种流程层面的过拟合形式，并询问具体的行为模式如何镜像经典APR中看似合理但不正确的补丁问题。

answer.en: The AgentLens framework's 'Lucky Pass' concept directly parallels the overfitting problem in classical APR by showing that a patch can pass all tests (the binary 'pass' signal) while being produced through a 'chaotic trial-and-error process' rather than a principled solution. The paper states that 'this outcome-only view treats a principled solution and a chaotic trial-and-error process as equivalent' and finds that '10.7% exhibit behavior we call a Lucky Pass.' Specific behavioral patterns identified that are analogous to plausible-but-incorrect patches include 'regression cycles' (reverting and re-applying changes without progress), 'blind retries' (repeating the same failing action), 'missing verification' (not checking whether a change actually fixes the intended issue), and 'temporally disordered exploration, implementation, and verification' (mixing up the logical order of debugging steps). These patterns mirror the classical APR problem where a patch passes the test suite but does not actually fix the underlying bug, because the agent's process is not genuinely solving the problem but rather stumbling into a passing state through chaotic, overfitted behavior.
answer.zh: AgentLens框架的“幸运通过”概念直接类比了经典APR中的过拟合问题，它表明一个补丁可以通过所有测试（二元“通过”信号），但却是通过“混乱的试错过程”而非原则性解决方案产生的。论文指出“这种仅关注结果的视角将原则性解决方案与混乱的试错过程视为等同”，并发现“10.7%表现出我们称之为幸运通过的行为”。识别出的类似于看似合理但不正确补丁的具体行为模式包括：“回归循环”（无进展地回退并重新应用更改）、“盲目重试”（重复相同的失败动作）、“缺少验证”（不检查更改是否真正修复了预期问题），以及“探索、实现与验证在时间上无序”（混淆调试步骤的逻辑顺序）。这些模式镜像了经典APR问题，即补丁通过了测试套件但并未真正修复底层错误，因为智能体的过程并非真正解决问题，而是通过混乱、过拟合的行为偶然进入通过状态。

question.en: What implications does the AgentLens process-level evaluation have for improving patch validation in LLM-driven APR agents, particularly in distinguishing between a principled solution and a lucky pass that might not generalize to other test cases or real-world scenarios?
question.zh: AgentLens流程级评估对于改进LLM驱动的APR智能体中的补丁验证有何启示，特别是在区分原则性解决方案与可能无法泛化到其他测试用例或现实场景的幸运通过方面？

reason.en: This question targets the user's interest in patch validation and reliability of LLM-driven APR. It asks how the paper's findings can inform better validation techniques that go beyond binary test pass/fail, which is a central concern in APR to avoid overfitting and ensure patch correctness.
reason.zh: 这个问题针对用户对补丁验证和LLM驱动APR可靠性的兴趣。它询问论文的发现如何为超越二元测试通过/失败的更好验证技术提供信息，这是APR中避免过拟合和确保补丁正确性的核心关注点。

answer.en: AgentLens directly addresses the problem of distinguishing principled patches from lucky passes by introducing a process-level evaluation that goes beyond binary test outcomes. The paper states that "the quality score separates passing trajectories into Lucky, Solid, and Ideal tiers" and identifies that "10.7% exhibit behavior we call a Lucky Pass: regression cycles, blind retries, missing verification, or temporally disordered exploration, implementation, and verification." This means that a patch that passes tests can still be flagged as a Lucky Pass if its underlying process is chaotic or lacks proper verification, which implies it is unlikely to generalize. The framework further "decomposes Lucky Passes into five recurring mechanisms," providing concrete behavioral patterns that can be used to reject patches that are not principled. For patch validation, AgentLens offers a way to "ranked by quality score instead of pass rate," showing that some models "move by as many as five rank positions" when process quality is considered. This directly informs better validation by enabling practitioners to filter out patches that pass tests but exhibit wasteful or disordered behavior, thereby reducing the risk of overfitting to the test set.
answer.zh: AgentLens通过引入超越二元测试结果的流程级评估，直接解决了区分原则性补丁与幸运通过的问题。论文指出“质量评分将通过的轨迹划分为幸运、扎实和理想三个等级”，并识别出“10.7%表现出我们称之为幸运通过的行为：回归循环、盲目重试、缺少验证，或探索、实现与验证在时间上无序”。这意味着一个通过测试的补丁，如果其底层过程混乱或缺乏适当验证，仍可能被标记为幸运通过，这暗示它不太可能泛化。该框架进一步“将幸运通过分解为五种重复机制”，提供了可用于拒绝非原则性补丁的具体行为模式。对于补丁验证，AgentLens提供了一种“按质量评分而非通过率排名”的方法，显示当考虑流程质量时，某些模型“排名变动多达五位”。这通过使从业者能够过滤掉那些通过测试但表现出浪费或无序行为的补丁，直接为更好的验证提供了信息，从而降低了过拟合测试集的风险。

question.en: Given that AgentLens uses Prefix Tree Acceptor (PTA) references built from multiple passing trajectories, how could this approach be adapted to improve fault localization in APR pipelines, for example by identifying divergence points where an agent's exploration deviates from successful solution paths?
question.zh: 鉴于AgentLens使用从多个通过轨迹构建的前缀树接收器（PTA）参考，这种方法如何被调整以改进APR流水线中的故障定位，例如通过识别智能体的探索偏离成功解决方案路径的分歧点？

reason.en: This question connects the paper's methodology (PTA references, divergence points) to the user's interest in fault localization. It explores a potential cross-cutting application of the paper's process analysis to a core APR task, asking how trajectory-level analysis could inform where and why an agent's repair attempt goes wrong.
reason.zh: 这个问题将论文的方法论（PTA参考、分歧点）与用户对故障定位的兴趣联系起来。它探讨了论文的流程分析对核心APR任务的潜在交叉应用，询问轨迹级分析如何能揭示智能体修复尝试在何处以及为何出错。

answer.en: AgentLens's methodology can be adapted to improve fault localization in APR pipelines by using its Prefix Tree Acceptor (PTA) references and divergence point detection to pinpoint where an agent's exploration deviates from successful solution paths. The paper explicitly constructs "task-level Prefix Tree Acceptor (PTA) references" by "merging multiple passing solutions for the same task" (Abstract), which encode the canonical sequence of actions across successful trajectories. AgentLens then identifies "divergence points" where a trajectory's actions differ from this reference, and the dataset includes "divergence points" as annotations (Abstract). In an APR context, a failing trajectory could be compared against the PTA reference built from successful patches for the same bug; the first divergence point would directly indicate the step where the agent's exploration, implementation, or verification goes wrong. The paper's context-sensitive intent labeler further enriches this by classifying actions into "Exploration, Implementation, Verification, or Orchestration" (Abstract), so a divergence in, say, the Implementation phase would pinpoint the faulty edit. This directly maps to fault localization: the divergence point marks the location (file, function, or edit) where the agent's path first deviates from known successful strategies, narrowing the search for the root cause.
answer.zh: AgentLens的方法论可以通过使用其前缀树接收器（PTA）参考和分歧点检测来定位智能体的探索偏离成功解决方案路径的位置，从而改进APR流水线中的故障定位。论文明确通过“合并同一任务的多个通过解决方案”（摘要）构建了“任务级前缀树接收器（PTA）参考”，这些参考编码了成功轨迹中的规范动作序列。AgentLens随后识别“分歧点”，即轨迹动作与参考不同的位置，并且数据集包含“分歧点”作为注释（摘要）。在APR上下文中，可以将失败的轨迹与从同一错误的成功补丁构建的PTA参考进行比较；第一个分歧点将直接指示智能体的探索、实现或验证出错的步骤。论文的上下文敏感意图标注器通过将动作分类为“探索、实现、验证或编排”（摘要）进一步丰富了这一点，因此，例如在实现阶段的分歧将精确定位错误的编辑。这直接映射到故障定位：分歧点标记了智能体路径首次偏离已知成功策略的位置（文件、函数或编辑），从而缩小了根本原因的搜索范围。

question.en: How do the quality tiers (Lucky, Solid, Ideal) defined by AgentLens correlate with the likelihood that a generated patch is a genuine fix versus a plausible but incorrect patch, and does the paper provide any evidence linking process quality to patch correctness beyond the SWE-bench test suite?
question.zh: AgentLens定义的质量等级（幸运、扎实、理想）与生成的补丁是真正修复还是看似合理但不正确的补丁的可能性之间有何关联？论文是否提供了任何证据，将流程质量与SWE-bench测试套件之外的补丁正确性联系起来？

reason.en: This question directly addresses the user's concern about overfitting and patch correctness. It asks whether the paper's process quality scores can serve as a proxy for patch reliability, and whether there is any analysis of how Lucky Pass patches perform on additional validation (e.g., held-out tests, static analysis), which is crucial for APR evaluation.
reason.zh: 这个问题直接涉及用户对过拟合和补丁正确性的关注。它询问论文的流程质量评分是否可以作为补丁可靠性的代理指标，以及是否有任何关于幸运通过补丁在额外验证（例如，保留测试、静态分析）中表现的分析，这对于APR评估至关重要。

answer.en: The paper defines quality tiers (Lucky, Solid, Ideal) based on process-level assessment of passing trajectories, but it does not provide any evidence linking these tiers to patch correctness beyond the SWE-bench test suite. The abstract states that "the quality score separates passing trajectories into Lucky, Solid, and Ideal tiers" and that "among passing trajectories in this subset, 10.7% exhibit behavior we call a Lucky Pass: regression cycles, blind retries, missing verification, or temporally disordered exploration, implementation, and verification." However, the paper does not report any analysis of whether Lucky Pass patches fail additional validation (e.g., held-out tests, static analysis) or whether Solid/Ideal patches are more likely to be genuine fixes. The evaluation is entirely based on the same SWE-bench test suite that defines the pass/fail signal, so the tiers only characterize the process that led to a passing patch, not the patch's correctness beyond those tests. The paper explicitly notes that "this outcome-only view treats a principled solution and a chaotic trial-and-error process as equivalent" and shows they are "empirically false," but it does not validate the tiers against any external correctness measure. Therefore, the paper does not provide evidence that process quality scores serve as a proxy for patch reliability beyond the SWE-bench test suite.
answer.zh: 论文基于通过轨迹的流程级评估定义了质量等级（幸运、扎实、理想），但没有提供任何证据将这些等级与SWE-bench测试套件之外的补丁正确性联系起来。摘要指出“质量评分将通过的轨迹划分为幸运、扎实和理想三个等级”，并且“在通过轨迹中，10.7%表现出我们称之为幸运通过的行为：回归循环、盲目重试、缺少验证，或探索、实现与验证在时间上无序”。然而，论文没有报告任何关于幸运通过补丁是否未能通过额外验证（例如，保留测试、静态分析）或扎实/理想补丁是否更可能是真正修复的分析。评估完全基于定义通过/失败信号的同一SWE-bench测试套件，因此等级仅表征了导致通过补丁的过程，而非补丁在这些测试之外的正确性。论文明确指出“这种仅关注结果的视角将原则性解决方案与混乱的试错过程视为等同”，并表明它们在经验上是错误的，但它没有针对任何外部正确性度量来验证这些等级。因此，论文没有提供证据表明流程质量评分可以作为SWE-bench测试套件之外补丁可靠性的代理指标。

question.en: What lessons from the AgentLens study could inform the design of more robust APR agents that avoid 'Lucky Pass' behavior, and how might these insights be integrated with classical APR techniques (e.g., template-based repair, formal verification) to improve both process quality and patch reliability?
question.zh: 从AgentLens研究中可以汲取哪些教训来设计更稳健的、避免“幸运通过”行为的APR智能体？这些见解如何与经典APR技术（例如基于模板的修复、形式化验证）相结合，以改进流程质量和补丁可靠性？

reason.en: This question synthesizes the user's interest in both classical and LLM-driven APR, and asks for actionable insights from the paper that could guide future system design. It probes how the paper's findings on process quality could be combined with established APR techniques to reduce overfitting and improve industrial deployment readiness.
reason.zh: 这个问题综合了用户对经典和LLM驱动APR的兴趣，并询问论文中可指导未来系统设计的可行见解。它探讨了论文关于流程质量的发现如何能与已建立的APR技术相结合，以减少过拟合并提高工业部署的 readiness。

answer.en: The AgentLens study reveals that 10.7% of passing trajectories are 'Lucky Passes' characterized by 'regression cycles, blind retries, missing verification, or temporally disordered exploration, implementation, and verification.' To design more robust APR agents, these findings suggest integrating process-level checks that detect and penalize such chaotic behavior. For example, a system could combine classical template-based repair (which enforces structured patch generation) with AgentLens-style process monitoring: the agent's actions would be classified into 'Exploration, Implementation, Verification, or Orchestration' phases, and any deviation from a principled order (e.g., implementing before exploring the root cause) would trigger a warning or require re-execution. Formal verification could then be applied not just to the final patch but to intermediate states, ensuring that each step maintains logical consistency—directly countering the 'missing verification' pattern. The paper's 'Prefix Tree Acceptor (PTA) references' built from 'merging multiple passing solutions' offer a template for what a high-quality process looks like; classical APR could use such references to guide search or prune candidate patches that follow a 'Lucky Pass' trajectory. Concretely, an agent that attempts a 'blind retry' without verifying the fix would be flagged by the process monitor, and the system could fall back to a verified template or a formal proof obligation before accepting the patch.
answer.zh: AgentLens研究揭示，10.7%的通过轨迹是“幸运通过”，其特征是“回归循环、盲目重试、缺少验证，或探索、实现与验证在时间上无序”。为了设计更稳健的APR智能体，这些发现建议集成能够检测并惩罚此类混乱行为的流程级检查。例如，系统可以将经典的基于模板的修复（强制执行结构化的补丁生成）与AgentLens风格的流程监控相结合：智能体的动作将被分类为“探索、实现、验证或编排”阶段，任何偏离原则性顺序的行为（例如，在探索根本原因之前实现）将触发警告或需要重新执行。然后，形式化验证不仅可以应用于最终补丁，还可以应用于中间状态，确保每一步都保持逻辑一致性——直接对抗“缺少验证”模式。论文的“前缀树接收器（PTA）参考”通过“合并多个通过解决方案”构建，为高质量流程提供了模板；经典APR可以使用此类参考来指导搜索或修剪遵循“幸运通过”轨迹的候选补丁。具体来说，尝试“盲目重试”而未验证修复的智能体将被流程监控器标记，系统可以在接受补丁之前回退到经过验证的模板或形式化证明义务。
